summary: We are in a high-stakes push to launch the 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' joint report with IE in September, while simultaneously executing the Asia Start campaign with two parallel routes and finalizing the Intelligent China program website.
summaryDetail: The single most important initiative is the joint report with IE on Chinese companies going global to Southeast Asia, targeting a September launch at Tech in Asia in Singapore. This week, the team must finalize the report structure and promotion plan (Felix's one-pager), identify op-ed contributors, and begin translating the Chinese report into English (not exceeding 50%). Meanwhile, the Asia Start campaign has launched with two routes—AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy)—and needs close monitoring. The Intelligent China program website is being finalized by Joseph, and SCM campaigns will start once it's live. Urgently, the knowledge website redesign requires feedback on Joker's design (Joseph, Julie) and removal of the video section. Social media agency selection is also critical: Kayleigh must follow up on agencies, and TIAN Xuefang needs to prepare supplementary materials for the second round of bidding. Upcoming this week: Julie must send the Asia Start spotlight article, adjust program figures in the school brochure, and join the meeting with Lee on the Google program brochure. The team should also analyze existing Asia Star participant profiles to inform program strategy.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed two design proposals for the knowledge website, preferring Joker's version with improved menu categorization but wanting more differentiation from current layout. They discussed incorporating elements from Joseph's version to show more articles and indices.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, social media agency search, IE report translation, and various ongoing projects including brochures, videos, and press releases.

meetings.title: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global
meetings.summary: Meeting to plan a joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with a working title 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'. The report will feature research from Professor Leeway, op-eds from leadership, and case studies, aiming for a September launch at events in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), discussed new Intelligent China program targeting foreigners with three modules, and planned SCM campaign for the new program.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in shaping cooperation.

decisions.text: Keep the current knowledge website structure but revamp menu categorization.
Remove dedicated video section; videos will be dispersed by topic.
Adopt card-based design for different document types to improve UX.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; put US partnerships on hold initially.
Include Berkeley program as a third priority for audit phase.
Menu should be categorized by topics, highlighting professors, publications, and research.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu is needed, either incorporating the 10 points or turning it into a 10-point article.
IE report translation should not exceed 50% of the original Chinese report.
IE report will launch digitally if printing is not feasible, at Tech in Asia conference in Singapore on September 15.
The report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with joint distribution.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: The working title is 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: The report will include a main research feature from Professor Leeway, op-eds from leadership (President Santiago, Dean of IE), and industry perspectives (case studies or alum op-eds).
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Target launch in mid-September at events in Singapore (Tech in Asia or Tyson).
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Asia Start will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will be marketed as a deeper dive compared to Asia Start to avoid cannibalization.
Proceed with second round of social media ad agency bidding with supplementary materials
